author: @ClownWhisper
text: And if you're dealing with the very very tiny molex G version the tiny black or white three or four pin connector sometimes 2 pin they make a tool for it so you don't bend it up although I've never had to use the tool I always go over to a bench grinder and grind down a very tiny nail to the right size and keep it at my work bunch. With the molex connectors it's so easy to destroy the connector by pulling before the tab is pressed down so you have to be careful because once it's cracked you need a new connector

author: @ClownWhisper
text: PUSH PULL EVERY CONNECTION and ring them out if possible

author: @TheLightningStalker
text: Sometimes the little sprig gets bent a bit too far and you have to bend it back and hope it doesn't break.

author: @hommerdalor6301
text: Worst than Friday night, it is Saturday 2:30 morning 🌄

author: @iamfingazonelove7937
text: Would love to hear your breakdown on/of graphene in the jab..

author: @benniedonald
text:
Switched out the fuse box on my sister in-laws Chevy Aveo. It had burned out several fuse connections and destroyed the box. I picked out every pin in the burned out fuse connections and put new connections in a new fuse box. 
Lots of tedious work and soldering. I was glad I didn't have to do the entire box. With new plugs and fuses. LOL 😆

author: @larryniidji
text: For $10-$20 on Ebay or Amazon you can get "82Pcs Pin Ejector Wire Kit Auto Terminal Removal Connector Pin Extractors Tools". For all different connectors. Worth having on hand.

author: @AM-us7cz
text: Great video Rob. I have known for many years now that if it was made  or put together on Monday morning or Friday afternoon it will probably be dodgy. Ha ha sad fact of modern day life.

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
I make my own connectors often so knew this trick but it is surprising how many don't realise just how easy they are to remove.
Thanks Rob

author: @pulesjet
text: I worked in a wire harness building outfit for some three years.   I have all sorts of specialty tools for removing terminals in various connectors.  They can be purchased on line.

replies.author: @consaka1
replies.text: Can you recommend a good kit?

author: @pauldunn3322
text:
Re sodering????
Arn't you British? Here it's SOLDERING! lol. Please don't go down the American route.

replies.author: @nil5221
replies.text:
I picked that up too! But it was a slip of the tongue, Rob has said "solder" many times before (including in this video). 😁
American butchery of English is very strange! They change words like "colour" and "paediatric" to "color" and "pediatric" so that the spelling agrees with the pronunciation, but then pronounce "solder" as "sodder". Very strange.

author: @Opel_Guy
text: A good tip is when some crimps/tags are difficult to remove, push the wire first as this will push the crimp/tag up in the connector a bit and stop the tang/locking tab digging in to the plastic of the connector when trying to release it. Used this trick many times.

author: @wongowonga
text: On a different topic but very much your kind of thing, I was sat in the garden the other day and the parasol I was sat under started spinning in the wind. I then realised it could possibly be adapted as the frame for  VAWT. The ribs/trusses that hold the parasol up could fairly easily be covered with something to make a wind turbine (once the fabric was removed). It has the benefit of having a little height already too. Anyway, I thought of you as soon as I thought about it. Just thought I'd throw the idea out there, I may well have a tinker with it soon. Some extra rigidity would probably be needed but It may well be possible without too much messing...
